A couple of quick questions

Your answers are anonymous — DAN does not collect your name, email, or any identifying information. Your Regional Center selection helps DAN give you accurate guidance. Age and race/ethnicity are optional and help us understand who DAN is reaching and make the case for continued funding.

Please select your Regional Center to continue.

DAN

Disability Access Navigator

DAN helps you navigate California's Regional Center system -- what to ask for, how to ask for it, and what to do when the first answer is no. Free, confidential, available any time.

Before we start: DAN is an informational tool -- not legal advice. For your specific situation, you should seek guidance from a qualified advocate or attorney. Everything you share stays in this conversation -- nothing is stored or linked to you. Your feedback helps shape what DAN becomes.
→ DAN asks one question at a time. Click the button that fits — or type your own answer.
→ When the right next step is a written request, DAN drafts the email for you. Copy it and fill in the blanks.
→ Download your conversation before you close — nothing is saved between sessions.

By clicking Let's get started, you acknowledge that DAN provides general information only — not legal advice. DAN is not a lawyer or an authorized representative. For legal matters, consult a qualified attorney or disability rights organization.

Been here before?

DAN doesn't remember past sessions — but you can bring it up to speed. If you downloaded or copied your last conversation, paste it below and DAN will pick up where you left off.

Which of these sounds like your situation?

I want to ask for a service or prepare for an IPP meeting
Ask for a service, prep for a meeting, or paste your IPP for a goal-by-goal review.
Self-Determination Program (SDP)
Whether you're exploring SDP, getting started, building or revising your spending plan, or running into issues -- DAN can help.
RC said no -- and we're still talking, or they haven't responded
They denied something, reframed your ask, pointed to a community resource, or stopped responding.
I got a denial letter (Notice of Action / NOA)
A formal letter that starts a 60-day appeal clock -- DAN can walk through it with you and help you prepare your response.
Something else -- help me figure it out
Tell DAN what's happening in your own words and we'll sort it out together.
DAN

💾 Reminder: If you close this window, your conversation will be gone — download it to save it or pick up where you left off next time.
